reason: The statement applies because the sons of Jacob, Simeon and Levi, took action against the people of the land, specifically Shechem, due to the defilement of their sister Dinah. They were not willing to give their sister to an uncircumcised man, which would have been a reproach to them, and instead required the men of the city to be circumcised before agreeing to intermarriage. When the men of the city agreed and were circumcised, Simeon and Levi took advantage of their vulnerability and attacked, slaying the males of the city as a form of retribution. This action ultimately led to Jacob's concern about retaliation from the surrounding inhabitants, showcasing the consequences of the sons' actions.
